title: Collecting eDNA from marine water samples in the field
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2anit","text":"This protocol describes the process of collecting eDNA from marine water samples in the field and four different approaches for preservation of filters with eDNA as well as eDNA extraction: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"82fqv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"682lh","text":"Qiagen DNeasy kit","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4pqfl","text":"Zymo DNA/RNA shield","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fk639","text":"Longmire’s solution","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5q484","text":"Alternative methods","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"69a1l","text":"Remarks/Tips:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2oant","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a6a49","text":"Water filtering:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cbjfp","text":"The 250ml Nalgene unit is a nice size, but will clog quite rapidly if filtering lots of dirty water. You also need to empty and replace the bottom container more often, of just use a 1l (or 2l) scott/duran bottle as a replacement for the supplied 250ml bottle. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9kn8f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3b73m","text":"DNA Preservation method used:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":0,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4i44s","text":"For short preservation times, filters can be directly placed in ATL buffer (Qiagen DNeasy kit). This works well if you are able to process the samples within 48h or you have a freezer available within that time. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b4til","text":"For preservation longer than a few days, I tested Longmire’s solution. Works well, but only compatible with Phenol/Chlorophorm extraction, which is laborious and also does not yield the best and purest DNA. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6j1fn","text":"Now I switched to using Zymo DNA/RNA shield. Have no test results back, but expect good results, and major advantage is that it directly fits into both the Zymo Quick-DNA miniprep Plus kits as well as the Qiagen DNeasy kit. And DNA will be stable at RT for 1 month. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"b06rp","text":"Starting remarks:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3iv7g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4qsfi","text":"All non-DNA free/not-gamma sterilized sampling devices are cleaned with a bleach solution or UV exposure before use to break down contamination DNA. Always wear gloves and minimize potential contamination with DNA from human handling and/or nearby food sources or animals.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":55,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"am1mc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3dv14","text":"Collect at least one control sample on site of sampling collection. Filter 500 ml of DNA free Milli-Q (if not available, use tap water) at each location, to monitor contamination during the filtering and subsequent DNA extraction.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":20,"length":58},{"style":"bold","offset":79,"length":56}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"c93od","text":"Please refer to the SDS (Safety Data Sheet) for safety warnings and hazard information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"lJAjo","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"W6fYM","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"f4gc4","text":"Please refer to the steps for additional materials as each step-case requires different materials.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":205389,"guid":"41AEC182341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Thermo Scientific™ Nalgene™ Rapid-Flow™ Sterile Disposable Filter Units with CN Membrane","mol_weight":0,"sku":"10792351","url":"https://www.fishersci.co.uk/shop/products/nalgene-rapid-flow-sterile-disposable-filter-units-cn-membrane/10792351","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":9,"name":"Thermo Scientific","link":"https://www.lifetechnologies.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Collect water in an appropriate sterile, DNA free container. For marine samples collected during scuba diving, we use a small hand-pump and a punch-balloon.
Filter 1 L - 2 L with the 250 ml filters (0.8 micron, cellulose nitrate) from Nalgene.
Connect filter unit to vacuum pump, filter by vacuum. I use a small vacuum-pump from ebay, powered with a 12V power pack. (DC12V Mini Vacuum Pump Negative Pressure Suction Pump)
Using the vacuum, make sure the filter is dry, as to much transfer of liquid influences preservation and downstream DNA isolation.
Cut filter from filter unit, it is glued in place. No problem to cut off outer 0.5 cm of filter. Use sterile scalpel + scalpel holder. Fold filter twice using scalpel+tweezers.

Insert filter in 2 ml screwcap tube prefilled with 400 µL, preservation solution . It is possible to use other tubes, but not when using 3d-fuge, as these do not fit and will snap open during centrifugation.
Shake tube with filter, to fully soak in the preservation solution. Repeat after 00:02:00 - 00:03:00 to re-soak. Filter ready for storage.

The DNA is now collected on the filter. Either directly continue with the DNA isolation protocol of choice, or store the preserved the DNA on the filter, depending on preservation method used. Qiagen DNeasy kit, short term (<48h) preservation: Store filter in 400 µL, ATL buffer from the DNeasy kit, shake well, store at Room temperature or below. Zymo DNA/RNA shield, short+longterm preservation: Store filter in 400 µL, DNA/RNA Shield , shake well, store at Room temperature or below. Longmire’s solution: longterm preservation: Store filter in 500 µL - 1 mL, longmire’s solution , shake well, store at RT or below cheap, works ok, but only compatible with Phenol/Chlorophorm extraction, which is laborious and also does not yield the best and purest DNA. Other alternative preservation methods : ethanol, freezing, DMSO, DESS, dehydration, combinations, etc.
When in the lab, add 20 µL, protK .

Follow standard protocol for blood samples: Since using 400 µL volume, double amount of AL and Ethanol: add 400 µL, Buffer AL . Mix thoroughly by vortexing, add 400 µL, Ethanol . mix by vortexing, load on spin column, etc.. Pipetting is a bit of hassle with the filter in the tube, best to remove most buffer, spin shortly, remove rest. Also you will need to load the DNA filter tube twice to process all liquid. Elute in either recommended 200 µL, AE or use lower elution volume (down to 50 µL ) if more concentrated DNA is required.
Add 400 µL, Buffer AL .
Mix thoroughly by vortexing.
Add 400 µL, Ethanol .
Mix by vortexing.
Load on spin column.
In the lab, add 20 µL, ProtK .
Incubate at 56 °C for 00:10:00 - 00:30:00 .

Add 1 volume of genomic extraction buffer, follow protocol (mix by vortexing, load on spin column, etc.). Pipetting is a bit of hassle with the filter in the tube, best to remove most buffer, spin shortly, remove rest. Elute in either recommended volume ( 50 µL ) or use higher elution volume if more dilute DNA is required.

Use classic phenol/chlorophorm extraction, that works also very well. I did this with the samples I preserved in Longmire solution, can also be done with samples in DNA/RNA shield. Before doing DNA extraction incubate for 00:30:00 with protK at 56 °C .

With the larger filter units (500ml) you also get larger filters, which do not fit in a 2ml tube. Either use a 15ml tube, or the following idea will also work: cut the filter from the 500 ml units in 4 equal pieces and process these separately. Isolate DNA from 2 quarters, store the other 2 in Longmire for later if something goes wrong with the isolation.

If filters in 2ml tubes and volume <1ml, extraction can be done in the 2ml tubes, much easier. If filter preserved in more than 1 ml liquid, transfer to 15ml falcon tubes, or use the 15ml tubes with the filters. Optional when using 15ml tubes: Add several sterile, DNA free, 4mm glass-beads.
Add 30 µL, proteaseK .
Vigorously vortex the tube with filter+longmire solution.
Incubate for 00:30:00 – 02:00:00 at 56 °C - 60 °C , with regular vortexing.
Add 1 volume of Phenol/Chlorphorm/Isoamylalcohol (PCI) ( 2 mL - 3 mL usually).
Homogenize on vortex mixer or vortex for ~ 00:02:00 .
Spin down 00:10:00 at .
Waterphase now pink or more dark red (some samples). PCI yellowish, but not pink. Collect waterphase.
Second extraction with 2 mL - 3 mL, Chlorophorm/IAA . Spin for 00:10:00 at .
Collect ~ 2 mL - 3 mL of the waterphase.
Add 500 µL, NaAcetate 3M .
Precipitate 01:00:00 at -20 °C .
Invert some more for about 00:05:00 . Often a red-pinkish colour develops (probably some reaction between the algal chlorophyll and the PCI?)
Add 7 mL, cold EtOH . White precipitate forms, this is normal.
Spin down for 00:15:00 at . Sometimes yellowish sticky/slimy pellet visible(polysaccharides?), can be removed by washing with 70% EtOH. Necessary to resuspend the gooey pellet by shaking/vortexing.
Wash pellet in 3 mL, 70% EtOH . (1/2)
Wash pellet in 3 mL, 70% EtOH . (2/2)
Dry pellet to air.
Resuspend in 100 µL - 200 µL, NFW/T10E1/EB .
creator: Reindert Nijland
